title: Adjusting: Accrued Revenue
event: By December 31, Tran Consulting has performed $1,500 of services for a client that have not yet been billed or collected. Prepare the adjusting entry.

lines.label: earned, not yet billed
lines.why: The claim exists the moment the work is done — the invoice is paperwork, not the earning event.
lines.trap: Unbilled ≠ unearned. Don't wait for the invoice to recognize what's already been performed.

lines.label: work performed by 12/31
lines.why: December's work belongs in December's income.
lines.trap: Skipping this understates both revenue and assets — a classic exam 'find the missing adjustment.'

memorize.body: The fourth adjustment family: accrued revenue = work first, cash later. Mirror image of accrued salaries.
When cash arrives next month, the entry is Dr Cash / Cr Accounts Receivable — revenue does NOT repeat.

questions.prompt: $1,500 of services performed by Dec 31, billed in January. Revenue recognized at Dec 31 = ?

distractors.feedback: Billing timing doesn't control earning. The work is performed, so the revenue accrues at year-end.

build.scaffold: Work done + no cash yet = receivable and revenue. The invoice date is irrelevant.
